-- 1 of 4 --

Your Avatar, Part 1
We’re now going to talk about your Avatar, and this is some of the most important,
most powerful work you’ll do within this module and probably within the entire Product
Launch Formula.
Your avatar, is a composite representation of your ideal client. Who is your
average client? Sometimes you might have more than one avatar. For example, in
Product Launch Formula I have more than one avatar. I have people that already have
an existing online business and they want to leverage it. They want to get better
results.
I also have people that want to start an online business and are just starting out. Those
are two very different avatars. I've got more avatars - artists, authors, practitioners,
people with offline businesses that want to move online. So as you can see I have
several different avatars.
If you have more than one avatar (and you probably do) you might want to do this
exercise we're about to go through for each avatar. This is really powerful stuff. This
concept of being really clear on who you're trying to reach is super important.
It can be really helpful to think of your avatar as a real person. I know some
people recommend giving them a real name, and talk about them as if they're a specific
person. That can be really powerful because in your marketing you always want to be
talking to one person.
You don't want to be writing to your list or recording videos thinking you’re writing an
email to 6,000 people. No, you're writing that email for 6,000 individual people who are
going to go check their email, real individual people who are going to open up their
email program and see your email in their inbox. This is a very private place for them,
and they're going to read it to see if you connect with them.
It’s the same thing when people watch your video. You might be putting it out on
YouTube, or using a pre-launch video that lots of people will watch, but it’s individual
people watching it. Never forget that you're talking to one person.
A lot of times people talk like they’re going to “send out blasts”. “I'm going to blast my
list, I'm going to send a blast to my list”, and they're basically talking about email
broadcasts, but they'll use that word blast. Some people just use it and that's fine, but
we have a rule in my company with my team. We can't use that word, "blast." We do
not blast the people on our list. Do you like to get blasted?
This is more of a mindset idea, but you never want to blast your list. You're sending
emails to your individual subscribers. It's all about connecting with your
prospects and clients - that’s what PLF is all about at the end of the day.

-- 2 of 4 --

The first step in connecting with them is to understand who they are, and that's what
this whole process is about. I'm going to go through this quickly, but this is not a
process that you should go through quickly. You should spend some real time walking
through this process.
This is how you connect with your avatar. I've got a set of questions for you, it's going
to give you some real insights that will help you create your offer. It’ll be the fuel for
your launch, it'll help you create every piece of content for them - whether it's an email,
or a blog post, or a video etc. You need to picture who your avatar is. You have to get a
real clear picture of them in your mind's eye.
I want you to actually work on these questions right now…
Think about your avatar, your ideal client. Who you want to reach? The person you
want to be a hero to. What is their biggest source of pain? What keeps them
awake at night? Just sit there and think about that. What's their biggest source of pain?
What do they worry about when they lie awake at night?
What scares your avatar? This can be a little different. If we go back, source of
pain, what scares them, could be very, very similar but they could be different. What
scares your avatar?
Then what's the biggest danger that they don't even see yet? You probably
have greater insight into what their path or journey is, what their transformation in life
is going to be, and maybe they've got something coming and they don't even know
about that danger yet.
What are your avatar's greatest opportunities? What's out there for them? What
awaits for them? I think about PLF and your opportunities for you ahead of you, and
you might think OK, I just want to knock out this launch, and I want to focus on that. I
also want to focus on you having that launch, but then I can look into your future,
because I've already been there.
I started out and I just look at my journey and I know that my journey is your journey,
and I can think about the amazing things, not just in your first launch, but the launch
after that and the launch after that. The business that comes out of that, and then the
next business and all the connections you're going to have.
I can just think about these amazing opportunities that you don't even know about yet.
When you think about your avatar, what are the opportunities they don't even know
about yet that are just going to show up? People don't even know about the launch
echo when they first hear about PLF, but I know about it. I know about the positioning
that's ahead of them.
I want you to do the same thing. I want you to think about what their opportunities are
going forward. So go ahead, write that down now.

-- 3 of 4 --

What are your avatar's hopes and dreams? Who or what do they aspire to
be? What's just a huge dream for them?
As you work through these questions and spend some time, you're going to get really
clear on who your avatar is and that's going to allow you to connect with them through
everything you do - through your video, your copy, your blog posts, emails, and I can’t
even tell you how powerful this is.
This is what I've built my entire business on and is my secret, being able to
truly connect with people. It's the basic ingredients of how you connect with your
prospects and your clients.
